blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: He mentioned he hadn't been sleeping well lately. A few weeks ago. We were on the phone and he said he'd been having trouble.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: It came up. We were talking about something else and it just came up. He said it wasn't a big deal.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: He never mentioned that to me.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: He probably didn't want to make it a thing. You know how he was about that. About asking for anything.
A pause. Everyone did know that.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: I don't know. I'm just saying what he told me.
It was a small thing. A reasonable thing to say. The kind of detail a grieving friend would offer to make sense of something that made no sense.
Wren heard it. Filed it away. Said nothing.
Nadia picked up her water glass and held it in both hands without drinking. Her eyes moved to the ceiling — the bedroom above them — and then back down. A second. No more.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: There was an argument. Tonight. We all heard it through the ceiling.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Right. But with who. That's going to be one of the first things they ask.
